content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://orbilu.uni.lu/handle/10993/63435", "source_title": "EU legislative approaches to the regulation of digitalisation", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "A second feature of much of the current legislation in digital matters is that obligations imposed therein require increased attention to in...", "direct_quote": "A second feature of much of the current legislation in digital matters is that obligations imposed therein require increased attention to information management \u2013 from sourcing to use, dissemination, and sharing.", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "This is a requirement for both public and private actors imposing ever more \u2018granular\u2019 knowledge and reporting of information flows in econo...", "direct_quote": "This is a requirement for both public and private actors imposing ever more \u2018granular\u2019 knowledge and reporting of information flows in economic operators.", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 2 facts."}]}
